title: Expected value
short: The long-run average of a random variable: the sum of each value times its probability.
full:
The expected value E(X) of a discrete random variable is the average you would expect over very many trials. It is computed as E(X) = Σ x·P(x), summing each possible value times its probability.

Expected value is the 'centre of mass' of a probability distribution. For a fair game the expected gain is zero; for the binomial distribution the expected number of successes is n·p.
